content:
Just now

--

We all know that a degree isn't a golden ticket to a job anymore.
Spending years studying doesn’t always pay off, and many graduates end up experiencing a career mismatch—working in fields totally unrelated to their studies.
Others face underemployment, taking on basic jobs that don’t utilize their qualifications.
Climbing the ladder in your exact field of study can be tough.
That’s why many professionals turn to freelancing, continuing their education, or going into teaching.
Another great way to stand out is through content creation—sharing insights on platforms like Dev.to—or even authoring books to build credibility and thought leadership in your niche.
What other strategies or approaches have helped you navigate your career path beyond a traditional degree?
I’d love to hear your experiences in the comments below!
